name: Ring-tailed Lemur
lifespan: 16–19 years in the wild; over 25 years in captivity
size: Body length 39–46 cm; tail 56–63 cm; weight 2.2–3.5 kg

name: Aye-aye
lifespan: 20–23 years in the wild; longer in captivity
size: Body length 36–44 cm; tail 56–61 cm; weight 2–3 kg

name: Meerkat
lifespan: 6–8 years in the wild; up to 13 years in captivity
size: Body length 25–35 cm; tail 17–25 cm; weight 0.6–1 kg

name: Caracal
lifespan: 10–12 years in the wild; up to 19 years in captivity
size: Body length 60–105 cm; tail 20–35 cm; weight 8–20 kg

name: Serval
lifespan: 10–12 years in the wild; up to 20 years in captivity
size: Body length 67–100 cm; tail 24–45 cm; weight 7–18 kg

name: Pangolin
lifespan: 10–20 years, exact wild lifespan poorly known
size: Body length 30–100 cm depending on species; weight 2–35 kg

name: Musk Ox
size: Shoulder height 1.1–1.5 m; weight 180–410 kg

name: Pronghorn
size: Shoulder height 80–100 cm; weight 35–70 kg

name: Bighorn Sheep
size: Shoulder height 80–100 cm; weight 50–140 kg

name: American Bison
size: Shoulder height 1.5–2 m; weight 300–1,000 kg

name: Tapir
lifespan: 20–30 years in the wild and captivity
size: Body length 1.8–2.5 m; weight 150–320 kg

name: Capybara
size: Body length 1–1.3 m; weight 35–66 kg

name: Howler Monkey
size: Body length 40–70 cm; tail 50–75 cm; weight 4–10 kg

name: Spider Monkey
lifespan: 20–25 years in the wild; longer in captivity
size: Body length 35–66 cm; tail 50–90 cm; weight 6–11 kg

name: Coati
lifespan: 7–8 years in the wild; up to 15 years in captivity
size: Body length 40–70 cm; tail 32–69 cm; weight 3–8 kg

name: Kinkajou
lifespan: 20–25 years in the wild; over 30 years in captivity
size: Body length 40–60 cm; tail 40–60 cm; weight 1.5–4.5 kg

name: Vicuña
size: Shoulder height 75–85 cm; weight 35–65 kg

name: Marmot
size: Body length 30–70 cm; weight 2–8 kg

name: Lynx
size: Body length 70–130 cm; weight 8–30 kg

name: Armadillo
size: Body length 15–100 cm depending on species; weight 85 g to over 50 kg

name: Numbat
size: Body length 17–27 cm; tail 13–17 cm; weight 280–700 g

name: Quokka
size: Body length 40–54 cm; tail 25–30 cm; weight 2.5–5 kg

name: Cassowary
lifespan: 18–20 years in the wild; longer in captivity
size: Height 1.2–1.8 m; weight 25–70 kg

name: Kakapo
size: Body length about 58–64 cm; weight 1–4 kg

name: Kea
lifespan: 10–15 years in the wild; over 20 in captivity
size: Body length 46 cm; wingspan around 90 cm; weight 0.8–1 kg

name: Shoebill
size: Height 1.1–1.5 m; wingspan 2.3–2.6 m

name: Hornbill
size: Body length 40 cm to over 1.2 m depending on species

name: Bee-eater
size: Body length 15–35 cm depending on species

name: Quetzal
size: Body length 36–40 cm; much longer including tail in males

name: Kiwi
size: Body length 35–65 cm; weight 1.2–3.3 kg

name: Ocean Sunfish
lifespan: Up to 10 years or more, exact lifespan uncertain
size: Length 1.8–3.3 m; weight 247–2,300 kg

name: Sailfish
size: Body length up to 3.4 m; weight up to 100 kg

name: Arapaima
size: Body length 2–3 m; weight up to 200 kg or more

name: Lionfish
size: Body length 20–45 cm

name: Leafy Seadragon
size: Body length 20–24 cm

name: Mudskipper
size: Body length 5–30 cm depending on species

name: Caecilian
size: Body length 10 cm to over 1.5 m depending on species

name: Glass Frog
size: Body length 2–8 cm

name: Newt
size: Body length 7–20 cm

name: Olm
lifespan: Over 50 years and possibly much longer
size: Body length 20–30 cm

name: Taipan
size: Body length 1.8–2.5 m

name: Frilled Lizard
size: Body length 70–90 cm including tail

name: Tuatara

name: Gaboon Viper

name: Gopher Tortoise

name: Mantis Shrimp

name: Giant African Millipede

name: Atlas Moth
size: Wingspan 24–30 cm

name: Goliath Beetle

name: Blue-ringed Octopus
